# ChatAPI

Self-hosted, open-source chat infrastructure for AI-powered apps. Single binary, SQLite, JWT auth. Think self-hosted Sendbird or Stream Chat — designed for apps where one or more participants is an AI.

Source: https://github.com/getchatapi/chatapi
Docs: https://docs.chatapi.cloud

## What ChatAPI is

ChatAPI is a Go binary that gives your app:
- Real-time messaging over WebSocket (DM, group, channel rooms)
- AI bots as first-class room participants
- LLM response streaming token-by-token (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, any OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
- JWT authentication — your backend signs tokens, ChatAPI validates them
- At-least-once delivery with retry for offline users
- Topic-based push notifications over WebSocket
- Room metadata — attach arbitrary JSON to rooms (order IDs, ticket IDs, listing IDs, etc.)

ChatAPI is NOT an LLM framework. It is the real-time messaging layer between your AI agent and your users.

## Authentication

All REST endpoints except GET /health and GET /metrics require:
  Authorization: Bearer <jwt>

JWTs must be HS256 signed using JWT_SECRET. The `sub` claim is the user ID.

WebSocket connections accept:
- Browser: wss://host/ws?token=<jwt>
- Server: Authorization: Bearer <jwt> header during upgrade

There are no API keys, no master keys, no sessions, no POST /ws/token endpoint.

## Configuration (environment variables)

JWT_SECRET          — required. HS256 signing secret.
LISTEN_ADDR         — default :8080
DATABASE_DSN        — default file:chatapi.db?_journal_mode=WAL&_busy_timeout=5000
ALLOWED_ORIGINS     — comma-separated CORS/WS origins. Use * for local dev.
WS_MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_USER — default 5
WORKER_INTERVAL     — default 30s
SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT — default 10s

## REST API

Authentication: Authorization: Bearer <jwt> on all endpoints except /health and /metrics.
Error shape: {"error": "code", "message": "human message"}

### Health
GET /health → {"status":"ok","db_writable":true}  — 503 if DB unreachable
GET /metrics → {"active_connections":N,"messages_sent":N,"broadcast_drops":N,"delivery_attempts":N,"delivery_failures":N,"uptime_seconds":N}

### Rooms
GET    /rooms                          — list rooms the current user belongs to
POST   /rooms                          — create room. Body: {"type":"dm"|"group"|"channel","members":["id1","id2"],"name":"optional","metadata":"optional json string"}. DMs are deduplicated by member pair.
GET    /rooms/{room_id}                — get room. 403 if not a member.
PATCH  /rooms/{room_id}               — update name or metadata. Body: {"name":"new","metadata":"json"}
GET    /rooms/{room_id}/members        — list members → {"members":[{"user_id":"...","role":"member","joined_at":"..."}]}
POST   /rooms/{room_id}/members        — add member (or bot). Body: {"user_id":"..."}
DELETE /rooms/{room_id}/members/{user_id} — remove member

### Messages
GET  /rooms/{room_id}/messages?after_seq=N&limit=50 — get messages. after_seq defaults to 0. limit max 100.
POST /rooms/{room_id}/messages         — send message. Body: {"content":"...","meta":"optional json string"}. Returns: {"message_id":"...","seq":N,"created_at":"..."}
PUT  /rooms/{room_id}/messages/{message_id} — edit message (sender only). Body: {"content":"..."}
DELETE /rooms/{room_id}/messages/{message_id} — delete message (sender only)

### Acknowledgments
POST /acks  — Body: {"room_id":"...","seq":N}  — marks messages delivered up to seq

### Notifications
POST /notify — send notification. Body: {"topic":"...","payload":{...},"targets":{"user_ids":["..."],"room_id":"...","topic_subscribers":true}}
POST /subscriptions    — subscribe current user to topic. Body: {"topic":"..."}
GET  /subscriptions    — list current user's subscriptions
DELETE /subscriptions/{id} — unsubscribe

### Bots
POST   /bots           — register bot. Body: {"name":"...","mode":"llm"|"external","provider":"openai"|"anthropic","base_url":"optional","model":"...","api_key":"...","system_prompt":"...","max_context":20}
GET    /bots           — list bots
GET    /bots/{bot_id}  — get bot
DELETE /bots/{bot_id}  — delete bot

After registering a bot, add it to a room via POST /rooms/{room_id}/members with {"user_id":"<bot_id>"}.

### Admin
GET /admin/dead-letters — view failed undelivered messages and notifications

## WebSocket API

Connect: wss://host/ws?token=<jwt>

All frames are JSON with a `type` field.

### Client → Server

send_message:   {"type":"send_message","data":{"room_id":"...","content":"...","meta":"optional"}}
ack:            {"type":"ack","data":{"room_id":"...","seq":N}}
typing.start:   {"type":"typing.start","data":{"room_id":"..."}}
typing.stop:    {"type":"typing.stop","data":{"room_id":"..."}}
ping:           {"type":"ping"}

### Server → Client

message:              {"type":"message","room_id":"...","seq":N,"message_id":"...","sender_id":"...","content":"...","meta":null,"created_at":"..."}
message.stream.start: {"type":"message.stream.start","room_id":"...","message_id":"...","sender_id":"..."}
message.stream.delta: {"type":"message.stream.delta","room_id":"...","message_id":"...","delta":"token chunk"}
message.stream.end:   {"type":"message.stream.end","room_id":"...","message_id":"...","content":"full text","seq":N}
ack.received:         {"type":"ack.received","room_id":"...","seq":N,"user_id":"..."}
presence.update:      {"type":"presence.update","user_id":"...","status":"online"|"offline"}
typing:               {"type":"typing","room_id":"...","user_id":"...","action":"start"|"stop"}
notification:         {"type":"notification","notification_id":"...","topic":"...","payload":"json string","timestamp":N}
server.shutdown:      {"type":"server.shutdown","reconnect_after_ms":N}

After reconnect, fetch missed messages: GET /rooms/{id}/messages?after_seq=<last_known_seq>

## TypeScript SDK

npm install @getchatapi/chatapi-sdk

```typescript
import { ChatAPI } from '@getchatapi/chatapi-sdk';

const client = new ChatAPI({ baseURL: 'https://your-chatapi.com', token: '<jwt>' });
await client.connect();

// Rooms
const room = await client.rooms.create({ type: 'dm', members: ['alice', 'bot_id'] });
await client.rooms.addMember(room.room_id, 'charlie');

// Messages
client.sendMessage(room.room_id, 'Hello');                         // WebSocket
await client.messages.send(room.room_id, 'Hello');                 // REST
const history = await client.messages.get(room.room_id, { after_seq: 0 });

// Events
client.on('message', (e) => console.log(e.content));
client.on('message.stream.start', (e) => { /* bot started */ });
client.on('message.stream.delta', (e) => process.stdout.write(e.delta));
client.on('message.stream.end', (e) => console.log(e.content));
client.on('presence.update', (e) => console.log(e.user_id, e.status));

// Bots
const bot = await client.bots.create({ name: 'Assistant', mode: 'llm', provider: 'openai', model: 'gpt-4o', api_key: 'sk-...', system_prompt: 'You are helpful.' });
await client.rooms.addMember(room.room_id, bot.bot_id);

// Notifications
await client.subscriptions.subscribe('order.updates');
client.on('notification', (e) => console.log(JSON.parse(e.payload)));
```

## LLM bot streaming flow

1. User sends a message to a room that has an llm-mode bot as a member
2. ChatAPI fetches the last max_context messages as conversation history
3. ChatAPI calls the LLM provider (streaming if supported)
4. Tokens are broadcast as message.stream.delta events to all online room members
5. When complete, the full message is stored and message.stream.end is broadcast with seq
6. Clients that missed the stream can fetch the completed message via GET /rooms/{id}/messages?after_seq=N

## Typical integration pattern (AI customer support widget)

Backend:
1. On widget open, mint a JWT: sub = session or user ID, signed with JWT_SECRET
2. Call POST /rooms with type=group, members=[userId, botId], metadata={"page":"/pricing"}
3. Return {token, room_id} to the frontend

Frontend:
1. new ChatAPI({ baseURL, token })
2. await client.connect()
3. Listen for message and message.stream.* events to render chat
4. Call client.sendMessage(room_id, userInput) on submit

## Scaling

Single instance (default): SQLite WAL mode, no external dependencies.
PostgreSQL: Implement repository interfaces in internal/repository/postgres/ — no service changes.
Horizontal: Implement broker.Broker backed by Redis pub/sub in internal/broker/redis/ — no service changes.
